steps.title: Identify the scientific use
steps.content: Modern birds have air-sac structures like those inferred for extinct theropods and sauropods. Paleontologists therefore use birds' respiratory systems as stand-ins for the extinct animals' systems.

steps.title: Choose the stand-in word
steps.content: "Proxies for" means substitutes or stand-ins, matching that scientific use. The extinct animals cannot be observed directly, so the modern bird structures serve as comparable evidence. A means forerunners, B means representatives or messengers, and C means lower-ranking things, none of which fits. The answer is D.
